title: Consolidate header into single unified bar with proper spacing

requirements.text: Merge the two separate header sections (tagline bar and navigation bar) into a single unified, cohesive header bar. All existing content must be preserved: the icon/logo, the tagline text (the span at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/header[1]/div[1]/p[1]/span[1]), navigation links, and any other current header elements. The result must appear as one header strip, not two stacked rows.

quotes: I just need it to be all in one head or not two Shepherd spots the way it is
I like everything that's set on the header and I like the icon and I like the image. Keep all that on there

acceptanceCriteria: The header renders as a single horizontal bar with no visible second bar or sub-bar
The logo/icon, tagline text, and navigation links are all present in the unified header
The header does not visually appear as two separate sections

requirements.text: Ensure the unified header does not overlap or block any page content below it. The hero section (and all other content) must start below the header with adequate clearance so nothing is hidden behind the header bar.

quotes: make the header fit better on the website don't block anything

acceptanceCriteria: No page content is visually hidden or overlapped by the header
The hero section begins below the full height of the header with visible separation

requirements.text: Retain the existing hide-on-scroll-down behavior for the unified header. The header should slide out of view when the user scrolls down and reappear when scrolling back up, exactly as currently implemented via the useScrollDirection hook.

quotes: have the header go away when I scroll down the same that it is

acceptanceCriteria: The header hides (slides up or fades out) when the user scrolls down
The header reappears when the user scrolls up
Behavior matches the existing scroll-direction logic

requirements.text: Add sufficient top margin or padding-top to the hero section so that the hero image is not too close to the header. There should be a comfortable visual gap between the bottom of the unified header and the top of the hero image.

quotes: the image was a little too close to the header

acceptanceCriteria: The hero image has a noticeable gap below the header and does not feel cramped against it
The spacing looks intentional and consistent with the site's visual style

constraints: Do not remove or replace the logo/icon in the header
Do not remove or change the tagline text content in the header
Do not remove existing navigation links from the header
Keep all other page sections (Hero, Menu, Catering, Contact, Footer) unchanged
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/
nonGoals: Changing the header's color scheme or typography
Adding new navigation items or header functionality
Modifying any section other than the header and hero top spacing
